01_initial_request.input_data: Exosomal cargo (lipids, proteins, mRNAs, miRNAs) from central nervous system cells and lysosomal storage disorder models
01_initial_request.user_intent: To understand exosomal content and function for utility in the treatment of specific lysosomal storage disorders and Parkinson disease
01_initial_request.quantifiable_goal: Development of new therapeutics for Parkinson disease and lysosomal storage disorders

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Exosomes are small membrane-bound organelles formed from endosomal membranes that can carry cargo between cells. [Gap] Need to isolate exosomes from relevant cell types to study their content and function in LSDs. [Decision] To achieve exosome isolation, chose to use cell culture methods to perform exosome purification on CNS cells and LSD model cells, expecting to obtain purified exosome populations.

parameters.cell_types: astrocytes, neurons, oligodendrocytes, microglia, fibroblasts, kidney tubule cells

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Exosomes isolated from various cell types including astrocytes, cortical neurons, oligodendrocytes, and patient-derived fibroblasts

references.relevance: Review describes exosome isolation from multiple CNS cell types and LSD models

references.citation: You et al., 2020, J. Extracell Vesicles 9, 1706801
references.relevance: Astrocyte-derived extracellular vesicles isolation from human primary astrocytes

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Exosome cargo includes proteins, lipids, mRNAs, and miRNAs that reflect cell metabolic state. [Gap] Need to characterize the molecular content of isolated exosomes. [Decision] To achieve cargo characterization, chose to use Mass Spectrometry to perform proteomic profiling on exosome samples, expecting to obtain protein composition data.

parameters.markers_detected: GFAP, SLC1A3/GLAST, SLCA1/GLUT1, GluR2/3, AMPARs, L1, PLP, CNP, MOG, MBP

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Proteomic profiling identified cell-type specific markers and cargo proteins in exosomes from astrocytes, neurons, and oligodendrocytes

references.citation: Faure et al., 2006, Mol. Cell Neurosci. 37, 642-648
references.relevance: Mass spectrometry evaluation of exosomes from rat cortical neurons

references.citation: Kramer-Albers et al., 2007, Proteom. Clin. Appl. 1, 1446-1461

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Exosome size and morphology can vary in disease states. [Gap] Need to quantify exosome physical properties in LSD patients. [Decision] To achieve size characterization, chose to use Dynamic Light Scattering to perform size distribution analysis on patient plasma exosomes, expecting to obtain exosome size profiles.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: DLS detected three distinct populations of EVs, with proportion of exosomes around 100 nm diameter significantly increased in GD cohort

references.citation: Tatiana et al., 2020, Eur. J. Med. Genet. 63, 104038
references.relevance: Altered level of plasma exosomes in patients with Gaucher disease using DLS

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Exosome morphology may be altered in disease states. [Gap] Need to visualize exosome structure at high resolution. [Decision] To achieve morphology visualization, chose to use Cryo-EM to perform high-resolution imaging on patient-derived exosomes, expecting to obtain structural images.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: High resolution images exhibited multilayered vesicles of larger size when compared to controls

references.citation: Tatiana et al., 2020, Eur. J. Med. Genet. 63, 104038
references.relevance: Cryo-EM showed abundance of vesicles with altered morphology in GD samples

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Lysosomal dysfunction may enhance exocytosis in LSDs. [Gap] Need to measure lysosomal enzyme release to assess exocytosis. [Decision] To achieve exocytosis measurement, chose to use Enzyme Activity Assay to perform NAG cleavage product measurement on fibroblast cultures, expecting to obtain exocytosis rates.

parameters.measurement: cleavage product of soluble lysosomal enzyme

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Amount of NAG released from control fibroblast cell lines was 250-fold higher than amount released in MLIV patient lines

references.citation: LaPlante et al., 2006, Mol. Genet. Metab. 89, 339-348
references.relevance: Lysosomal exocytosis impaired in MLIV measured by NAG release

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Lipid composition is important for exosome function and LSD pathology. [Gap] Need to analyze lipid content of exosomes. [Decision] To achieve lipid analysis, chose to use Thin Layer Chromatography to perform lipid extraction analysis on oligodendrocyte exosomes, expecting to obtain lipid composition data.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Identified enrichment of canonical myelin lipids galactocerebroside and sulfatide

references.citation: Kramer-Albers et al., 2007, Proteom. Clin. Appl. 1, 1446-1461
references.relevance: Lipid extracts from oligodendrocyte exosomes analyzed by TLC

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Exosomes can be engineered to carry therapeutic enzymes. [Gap] Need to load enzymes into exosomes for therapeutic delivery. [Decision] To achieve enzyme loading, chose to use Exosome Engineering to perform glucocerebrosidase fusion protein loading on HEK293-derived exosomes, expecting to obtain enzyme-loaded exosomes.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Exosomes were taken up by recipient cells, which showed significantly enhanced glucocerebrosidase activity

references.relevance: Targeted delivery of lysosomal enzymes using engineered extracellular vesicles

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Alternative enzyme loading methods may be needed for different therapeutic enzymes. [Gap] Need to test different loading techniques for TPP1 enzyme. [Decision] To achieve TPP1 loading, chose to use Sonication and Saponin Permeabilization to perform enzyme loading on naïve EVs, expecting to obtain TPP1-loaded EVs.

parameters.loading_method: sonication or saponin permeabilization ex vitro

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Both techniques successfully incorporated enzymatically active TPP1 into EVs, around 70% of enzyme delivered to lysosomes

references.citation: Haney et al., 2019, Adv. Healthc. Mater. 8, e1801271
references.relevance: TPP1 delivery to lysosomes with extracellular vesicles in Batten disease model

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Therapeutic exosomes need to be tested in vivo for efficacy. [Gap] Need to validate therapeutic effect in animal models. [Decision] To achieve in vivo validation, chose to use Mouse Models to perform intraperitoneal administration of EV-TPP1 on CLN2 mouse models, expecting to obtain therapeutic outcome data.

parameters.model: late-infantile neuronal ceroid lipofuscinosis mouse model

02_agent_trajectory.observation: EVs were delivered to the brain, and noted an increased lifespan after intraperitoneal administration of EV-TPP1

references.citation: Haney et al., 2019, Adv. Healthc. Mater. 8, e1801271
references.relevance: In vivo testing in CLN2 mouse model showing increased lifespan

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Genetic correction may restore normal exosome secretion patterns. [Gap] Need to test if restoring enzyme activity corrects exosome abnormalities. [Decision] To achieve functional rescue, chose to use Gene Transfection to perform wildtype NEU1 transfection on neu1-/- murine myofibroblasts, expecting to obtain corrected exosome release.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: When wild-type N-acetyl-α-neuraminidase activity was restored, exosomes isolated from rescued cell line no longer converted normal fibroblasts into myofibroblasts

references.citation: van de Vlekkert et al., 2019, Sci. Adv. 5, eaav3270
references.relevance: Restoring NEU1 activity corrected excessive release of exosomes carrying profibrotic signaling molecules

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Protein markers can confirm exosome identity and lysosomal involvement. [Gap] Need to detect exosome and lysosomal markers at plasma membrane. [Decision] To achieve marker detection, chose to use Western Blot to perform LAMP-1 and exosome marker detection on cell membranes, expecting to obtain protein expression levels.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Loss of MLN1 led to 50% reduction in levels of lysosomal marker LAMP-1 at plasma membrane; exosomal markers CD9, CD81, CD63, Hsp70 reduced upon MLN1 depletion

references.citation: Kim et al., 2019, Biochem. Biophys. Res. Commun. 510, 409-415
references.relevance: Exosomal release through TRPML1-mediated lysosomal exocytosis

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Exosome uptake by recipient cells needs to be characterized. [Gap] Need to understand cell-specific exosome uptake patterns. [Decision] To achieve uptake characterization, chose to use Cell Uptake Assays to perform exosome internalization studies on neurons and glial cells, expecting to obtain uptake specificity data.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Exosomes secreted by cortical neurons upon glutamatergic synapse activation were selectively endocytosed by neurons, whereas exosomes from neuroblastoma cells were taken up by glial and neuronal cells without bias

references.relevance: Emerging role of neuronal exosomes in CNS with cell-specific uptake

03_success_verification.validation_technique: Multiple experimental approaches including proteomic profiling, dynamic light scattering, cryo-EM, enzyme activity assays, and in vivo mouse models

exosome_size_increase.interpretation: Proportion of exosomes around 100 nm significantly increased in Gaucher disease cohort

NAG_release_reduction.interpretation: NAG released from MLIV patient fibroblasts was 250-fold lower than controls

LAMP1_reduction.interpretation: Loss of MLN1 led to 50% reduction in LAMP-1 levels at plasma membrane

enzyme_delivery_efficiency.interpretation: Around 70% of TPP1 enzyme was delivered to lysosomes using EVs

lifespan_outcome.interpretation: Increased lifespan noted after intraperitoneal administration of EV-TPP1 in CLN2 mouse model

03_success_verification.final_verdict: The research objective to understand exosomal content and function for utility in treatment of lysosomal storage disorders and Parkinson disease is supported by multiple studies showing exosomes can serve as therapeutic delivery vehicles, cross the blood-brain barrier, and deliver functional enzymes to target cells. However, the review notes that exosome therapeutics for LSDs is still an emerging field with challenges to address including loading capacity, half-life, dosage, biodistribution, and immunological response before widespread implementation.
